channel.title: InsurTech Intelligence
channel.description: Noticias globales de insurtech, actualizadas cada 6 horas.

item.title: Senators Ask for Review of Tesla’s ‘Full Self-Driving’ Safety Data
item.description: senators are asking the nation’s traffic safety regulator to examine Tesla’s self-published crash statistics for its “Full Self-Driving” (FSD) driver-assistance system, following a Reuters investigation last month that found the EV maker was exaggerating its safety claims. National Highway Traffic Safety Administration (NHTSA) on Monday, citing the Reuters report and saying the analysis underlying Tesla’s FSD safety statistics is “weak and misleading,” creating “an urgent safety problem.” Related: Tesla’s Supervised Full Self-Driving Rollout to Begin Soon in Denmark The letter asks NHTSA to write back by July 7 to answer a series of questions, including whether the agency has evaluated Tesla’s FSD safety claims or asked for the underlying crash data used to make the claims. The senators also urge NHTSA to strengthen reporting requirements for companies using self-driving technology or advanced driver-assistance systems like Tesla’s FSD, saying the agency has no way of knowing whether “public safety claims bear any relationship to reality.” Tesla did not respond to requests for comment.

item.title: California Workers’ Comp Med Cost Claims Rise Due to Use of Unlisted Codes: Study
item.description: A growing share of treatment rendered to injured workers in California is billed under unlisted codes that fall outside fee schedule pricing controls, creating a new and increasingly important source of medical cost growth, according to a new study by the California Workers’ Compensation Institute (CWCI). The results indicate that while professional services and facility fees consistently accounted for about 70% of all California workers’ comp medical spending, the strongest inflationary pressures came from professional services billed under codes that are not listed in the state’s Official Medical Fee Schedule (OMFS), where prices often grew faster than applicable fee schedule benchmarks. The CWCI study, the second in a series on workers’ comp medical inflation, uses data from the Institute’s Industry Research Information System (IRIS) database to examine medical payments from 2017 through 2024.

item.title: Federal Judge Sends Bayer’s $7.25B Roundup Settlement Back to Missouri State Court
item.description: A federal judge on Wednesday returned Bayer’s proposed $7.25 billion settlement of Roundup lawsuits to the Missouri state court, overruling the objections of some cancer victims who said the state court could not bind a nationwide class of people who allege that the popular weedkiller caused their cancer. The settlement, proposed in February, seeks to resolve nearly all lawsuits alleging that the company’s widely used weedkiller Roundup causes cancer, through a new class action filed in the Missouri state court. U.S District Judge Henry Edward Autrey granted a motion by Bayer, concluding that the objecting plaintiffs did not have the power to transfer the case to federal court.

item.title: “We’ll Want Some Proof’: State Farm CEO’s Take on NY Auto Insurance Reforms
item.description: “We want some proof before we move too hard [or] too fast there,” State Farm Chief Executive Officer Jon Farney told an analyst who asked whether the company would change it business plans in New York a few weeks after New York lawmakers agreed to some reforms proposed by Governor Kathy Hochul as part of the state’s budget approval process. While State Farm publicly supported auto insurance reforms measures in New York this year, now that a package of changes has been written into the state’s statutes, the insurance giant is taking a measured business approach. Related article : NY Lawmakers Agree to Governor’s Auto Insurance Reforms in New Budget The exchange between Farney and Assured Research President William Wilt took place during an Executive Perspectives panel at the S&P Global Ratings 42nd Annual Insurance conference in early June in midtown Manhattan, with Farney reporting that State Farm is excited about New York’s “first step” toward improving a difficult situation for insurers.

item.title: Beyond the headlines: the unseen fight against financial crime
item.description: Like many of you, I have had cases that led to high-profile trials and newspaper headlines. Speech by Therese Chambers, joint executive director of enforcement and market oversight, delivered at the International Bar Association (IBA) Anti-Corruption Conference. Speaker: Therese Chambers, joint executive director of enforcement and market oversight Event: 22nd Annual IBA Anti-Corruption Conference Delivered: 17 June 2026 Note: This is a drafted speech and may differ from the delivered version.

item.title: Orion180 lifts reinsurance coverage by 36% to $1.15bn for 2026 hurricane season
item.description: Insurance solutions provider Orion180 has increased its reinsurance tower by 36% year-over-year, reaching $1.15 billion of limit for the 2026 hurricane season, while also increasing the number of catastrophic events covered, further strengthening the organisation’s claims paying ability for multiple events throughout the year. Last year, Orion180 grew its reinsurance tower by 31% year-over-year, reaching $845 million of limit for the 2025 hurricane season. With the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA) forecasting 8 to 14 named tropical storms, with 3 to 6 hurricanes, and between 1 and 3 intense hurricanes of Category 3 strength or greater for the 2026 season , Orion180 stated that its expanded reinsurance program reinforces its focus on preparedness, claims-paying resilience, and disciplined growth.

item.title: Tropical storm Arthur forms, the first of the Atlantic season. Rainfall the main threat
item.description: Tropical storm Arthur has become the first named storm of the 2026 Atlantic hurricane season today, forming near the middle Texas coast and expected to be relatively short-lived, with heavy rainfall and life-threatening flooding seen as the main threats. Tropical storm Arthur has formed out of a disturbance made up of the remnants of storm activity in the Pacific and over northern Mexico, and while it may not retain tropical storm status for long, inches of rainfall are expected over a very wide area as it tracks over Gulf states. You can track the tropics using our 2026 Atlantic hurricane season page, which will be updated when any tropical storm activity occurs and with the latest forecasts.

item.title: Aegon announces senior leadership changes and confirms New York as future corporate headquarters
item.description: Alongside the leadership announcement, Aegon confirmed that New York City has been chosen as the location of its future corporate headquarters. Aegon said the establishment of its headquarters in New York represents another step in its corporate transformation and reflects its intention to further strengthen its presence as a US-based financial services group. Aegon, the multinational life insurance, pensions, retirement and asset management group, has announced a series of leadership changes as it progresses plans to relocate its head office and legal seat from the Netherlands to the United States.

item.title: Fenchurch Advisory Partners to combine with Broadhaven Capital Partners
item.description: Fenchurch Advisory Partners, an independent investment banking advisory firm, has announced it has entered into a definitive agreement to combine with Broadhaven Capital Partners. The firm provides M&A, capital raising and strategic advisory services across financial technology, asset and wealth management, market infrastructure, and the broader financial services sector. Broadhaven’s leadership team will join as partners of the combined firm, and its co-founder and partner, Gerard von Dohlen, will join the board of directors.

item.title: QDB and SACE sign export credit reinsurance deal to back joint exports
item.description: Qatar Development Bank (QDB) and SACE S.p.A., Italy’s export credit agency and one of the world’s leading Export Credit Agencies (ECAs), signed an export credit reinsurance agreement. For SACE, the agreement marks its first participation in a fully Sharia-compliant reinsurance arrangement, underscoring the growing role of Islamic finance principles in international export credit cooperation. Through a streamlined structure, the agreement aims to support joint export contracts between Qatari and Italian companies, particularly in projects where companies from both countries supply goods or services to buyers outside their home markets.

item.title: Oklahoma Insurance Commissioner Race: Sullivan, Quinn Advance to Runoff
item.description: The race for the next Oklahoma insurance commissioner narrowed on Tuesday, as Bob Sullivan and Marty Quinn advanced out of the Republican primary to an Aug. As a legislator, Quinn served as Chairman of the Oklahoma Senate Insurance Committee. The insurance commissioner race comes as Oklahomans face some of the highest homeowner’s insurance costs in the nation.

item.title: Pennsylvania Workers’ Comp Ban on Self-Referrals Does Not Apply to Pharmacies
item.description: The Pennsylvania Supreme Court has ruled 5-2 that the law’s prohibition on self-referrals is limited to the eight enumerated categories of services listed in the plain language of the statute — a list that does not include prescription drugs or professional pharmaceutical services. The anti-referral provision in Pennsylvania’s workers’ compensation law does not prevent a provider from referring a patient to a pharmacy in which the provider has a financial interest. Despite that stipulation, the pharmacy contended that the treatments in question were not prohibited self-referrals because the anti-referral provision does not include a bar on self-referrals for prescription drugs and pharmaceutical services.

item.title: Michigan Man Charged With Setting Fire to Church
item.description: A Flint, Michigan man was charged last week in a criminal complaint with arson and other related offenses for setting fire to St. Mark Missionary Baptist Church in Flint, Michigan was intentionally set on fire. In the video, Chambers brought a gas can wrapped in a black trash bag to the church and used an ignition device to set the church on fire.

item.title: Allianz Partners’ nib deal signals major shift in Australian and New Zealand travel insurance distribution
item.description: The post Allianz Partners’ nib deal signals major shift in Australian and New Zealand travel insurance distribution appeared first on Insurtech Insights . Allianz Partners’ acquisition of nib Group’s travel insurance business marks a significant strategic move that could reshape the travel insurance landscape across Australia and New Zealand, strengthening the insurer’s position across both digital and traditional distribution channels. The transaction, announced earlier this month, will see Allianz Partners acquire nib’s travel insurance portfolio, including the well-known Travel Insurance Direct (TID) brand, alongside a 20-year white-labelled distribution agreement spanning Australia and New Zealand.

item.title: Aon launches AI platform to analyse reinsurance contracts and identify coverage gaps
item.description: The post Aon launches AI platform to analyse reinsurance contracts and identify coverage gaps appeared first on Insurtech Insights . Global professional services firm Aon has launched a new artificial intelligence-powered platform designed to help insurers and reinsurers rapidly analyse contract language, identify emerging exclusions, and strengthen reinsurance coverage strategies. “Contract AI enables our reinsurance brokers to quickly analyse market developments at scale, identify emerging coverage considerations and provide clients with timely, relevant advice to meet strategic goals.” The platform was developed by Aon’s Risk Capital Product Team as part of the company’s broader strategy to leverage artificial intelligence and advanced analytics across the insurance and reinsurance value chain.

item.title: Reinsurers Pivot to Data and AI Strategies
item.description: As rate momentum stabilizes, reinsurers must leverage data and AI to generate operational alpha beyond traditional cycle management. Hence, leading reinsurers need to refresh their strategy to deliver a trusted, intelligent, and perpetually adaptable data and AI ecosystem (“cycle management”) for the enterprise. To defend technical underwriting margins and achieve sustainable growth, reinsurers must now shift their focus from riding market cycles to generating true operational alpha.

item.title: Insurance's $7 Trillion Question
item.description: AI-driven data center growth is creating complex insurance challenges that extend far beyond traditional property coverage. McKinsey projects that companies will invest nearly $7 trillion in data center infrastructure globally by 2030, with more than 40% of that spending concentrated in the U.S. As a class, data centers present property insurance exposures that differ significantly from other commercial risks.

item.title: Nassau Financial invests in Trussed AI
item.description: Nassau Financial Group has made an investment in Trussed AI, a provider of AI governance and compliance solutions for regulated enterprises. Nassau’s investment reinforces the importance of this challenge and validates the need for scalable AI governance capabilities across regulated industries.” – Ajay Dankar, Co-founder and Chief Executive Officer of Trussed AI. The investment was made through the Nassau Reimagine program.

item.title: Centri makes its first acquisition
item.description: The deal closed June 12 and is Centri’s first acquisition. Centri Business Consulting has acquired Altair Associates , a Minneapolis-based underwriting and claims services firm serving the property/casualty insurance and reinsurance market. For Centri — an accounting and advisory firm — the move expands a growing insurance practice and brings in specialized underwriting and claim advisory capabilities that are hard to build from scratch.

item.title: EverQuote launches ChatGPT app for car insurance shopping
item.description: EverQuote has launched a ChatGPT app that allows users to compare car insurance options directly within ChatGPT. The launch adds EverQuote to a growing list of insurance and financial services companies experimenting with ChatGPT as a distribution channel. According to the app listing, the tool is designed to help consumers browse coverage options and evaluate insurers without leaving the ChatGPT interface.

item.title: Citi Exec Alleges She Was Fired After Raising Trump-Related Compliance Concerns
item.description: A former Citigroup C.N executive alleged in a lawsuit that the bank fired her in retaliation for identifying regulatory and compliance risks that, according to a Financial Times report on Tuesday, were related to Citi’s efforts to court U.S. The complaint filed in Brooklyn federal court on Monday is heavily redacted but includes allegations that the former executive was fired shortly after raising concerns about the internal process for potentially taking on a client. The lawsuit, filed under the pseudonym Jane Doe, said the former executive was targeted with a “sham” human resources investigation and fired after she flagged “compliance risks that she believed to violate federal securities laws and misled shareholders.” The former executive said she identified deficiencies in Citi’s internal controls for risk management, anti-money laundering, reputational risk, and data compliance.

item.title: Hacking Group Claims Major Hack of Novo Nordisk And Attempted Extortion
item.description: A cyber extortion group claimed on Tuesday to have stolen more than a terabyte of data from pharmaceutical giant Novo Nordisk and said it is exploring selling parts of the data after unsuccessfully demanding $25 million from the company. FulcrumSec, a cyber extortion group that emerged in October 2025, said in a long message posted to its website that it spent more than two months in Novo Nordisk’s networks stealing data. A Novo Nordisk spokesperson said in an email that the company “is aware of claims that data allegedly copied externally without authorisation from our systems has been published online.

item.title: GEICO’s Gecko Appeared on His First Podcast
item.description: The iconic GEICO Gecko appeared live and unscripted on his first podcast last week. “This is the first podcast I’ve ever been on, and I’ve enjoyed every second of it,” the Gecko said during the episode. On June 11, the character appeared as a real-time, AI-powered participant on the iHeartMedia podcast “Fudd Around and Find Out,” hosted by Azzi Fudd, the No.

item.title: AM Best: Data Centers Pose Risks Beyond What P/C Industry Has Experienced
item.description: “As data center development and construction spreads, the required insurance coverage is evolving, as it is currently beyond what the traditional property/casualty industry has previously experienced,” added David Blades, associate director, industry research and analytics, at AM Best. “With the number and size of AI data centers growing rapidly, it is critical that P/C insurers provide solutions to support these highly complex, high-valued projects,” said AM Best. According to the report, there are no low-level hazards when considering primary risks associated with the building and running of data centers.

item.title: Some States Forging Ahead With AI Regulations Despite Trump’s Warning
item.description: Six months after President Donald Trump warned states not to regulate artificial intelligence, they are increasingly doing just that. Congress has stalled on producing federal regulation of artificial intelligence as states forge ahead and scrutinize how chatbots interact with children, how AI systems are used by employers, and what developers must do to try to prevent an AI-caused catastrophe. Trump’s move to restrain states’ actions on AI drew criticism from members of both political parties and civil liberties and consumer rights groups who worried that banning state regulation would amount to a gift to AI giants, who enjoy little to no oversight.

item.title: I Sold My AI Startup Before Revenue: Here’s What Investors Missed — And Founders Shouldn’t
item.description: I sold my AI research company while I was qualifying as a lawyer in the U.K. Roughly 20 months later , when Thomson Reuters acquired us, it was the first time in its 170-year history it bought a company pre-revenue. Our published papers put the model among the best in the world at legal reasoning, and we trained it for a fraction of what the large labs were spending.

item.title: ‘This System Wasn’t Built For Me’: Black Founders Became Investors To Change Venture Capital
item.description: “This system wasn’t built for me, and I knew that from day one,” he reflects. “My focus quickly became about learning and understanding the game of venture capital,” he said. Editor’s note: This article is the second in a three-part series on the state of venture investment to Black-founded startups in 2026.

item.title: Verisk Synergy Studio, a major milestone that reflects risk landscape demands: Natarajan
item.description: Tropical Cyclone model, delivered via the firm’s new cloud-native Synergy Studio platform, represents a major milestone built on years of deliberate work, and according to Gayatri Natarajan, SVP of Product Management, the extensive effort behind the release directly reflects the evolving demands of the complex risk landscape the industry navigates. This is the foundation, and we’ll keep building.” Natarajan particularly highlighted how Verisk Synergy Studio delivers high performance, scalability, and flexibility, all of which help drive companies’ catastrophe risk management forward. “Combined with Verisk’s global suite of scientifically rigorous models that leverage a shared financial framework, Verisk Synergy Studio streamlines your data ingestion, executes your analyses with your business preferences, and gives your team access to richer insights for your operations, while preserving familiar processes and accelerating collaboration across your teams,” Natarajan continued.

item.title: Achmea raises Windmill III Re 2026-1 cat bond target to €100m, price guidance lowered
item.description: Achmea, the Netherlands headquartered European insurance group, has now raised its target for collateralized European windstorm reinsurance from the new Windmill III Re DAC (2026-1) catastrophe bond by one-third to €100 million, while the price guidance has been lowered, Artemis can report. Now, we’re told Achmea is aiming to upsize this new Series 2026-1 cat bond by one-third to provide another €100 million of capital markets backed reinsurance, while at the same time the price guidance has fallen to below the initial range that was offered. Achmea returned to the catastrophe bond market at the start of the month, initially seeking €75 million in collateralized European windstorm reinsurance protection from the capital markets through sponsorship of this Windmill III Re 2026-1 cat bond.

item.title: Everest launches Annapurna Re casualty sidecar with $600m target, anchored by Stone Point
item.description: Everest Group has announced the launch of Annapurna Re Ltd., the specialty re/insurers first casualty reinsurance sidecar, revealing an expectation of deploying $600 million of third-party capital through the structure, while Stone Point Insurance Solutions is the anchor investor backing the venture. has been launched in Bermuda as the firm’s first casualty sidecar structure, in partnership with Stone Point. Stone Point will also serve as the exclusive investment manager to the assets underpinning the subject casualty reinsurance business that the sidecar assumes.

item.title: Arch promotes Halgan to CEO of Global Reinsurance and Schmeiser to CEO of Global Mortgage
item.description: Bermuda-based global insurance and reinsurance company, Arch Capital Group Ltd., has promoted Jerome Halgan to Chief Executive Officer (CEO) of Arch Global Reinsurance Group, and elevated Michael Schmeiser to CEO of Arch Global Mortgage Group. “I am honored for this opportunity to continue building Arch’s global reinsurance platform side-by-side with some of the brightest minds in the industry,” said Halgan. In their new roles, both Halgan and Schmeiser continue to report to Maamoun Rajeh, President of Arch Capital.

item.title: Orion180 lifts reinsurance cover 36% to $1.15bn for 2026 hurricane season
item.description: Insurance solutions provider Orion180 Insurance has successfully completed its reinsurance programmes ahead of the June 2026 treaty renewals, securing $1.15 billion of capacity across its towers, a 36% year-on-year increase. NOAA’s Atlantic hurricane season outlook for 2026 includes a likelihood of 8-14 named storms, 3-6 hurricanes, and 1-3 major hurricanes. The reinsurance programme covers Orion180’s growing admitted and surplus lines and is backed by a global panel of 41 highly rated and diversified reinsurers.

item.title: Everett and Doy join Markel International’s Canada CTCS team
item.description: Markel International, the insurance operations within Markel Group Inc., has appointed Alisha Everett as Assistant Vice President, Contractors, Trades and Construction Services, and Nicholas Doy as Manager within the CTCS team, effective immediately. In her new position, based in Toronto, Everett will lead Markel’s CTCS strategy across Canada, overseeing underwriting execution and driving profitable growth within the Contractors and Trades segment. As Manager, also based in Toronto, Doy will be supporting the CTCS’ team’s underwriting performance, as well as its portfolio development, and broker engagement across key regions in Canada.

item.title: Older, Wealthier Renters Drive Changes in Insurance Needs
item.description: As the demographic of the average American renter changes, so too do their insurance needs. Are they cross-selling life insurance to those renters?” With homeownership out of the picture for many older renters, insurers should be building out to bring those consumers along the lifetime value journey, Foy said. So those are all things that increase their insurance needs.” Umbrella policies are a natural cross-sell to renters, as is small business coverage, Foy said.

item.title: Viewpoint: The Upcoming World Cup and Injury Risk
item.description: AI is increasingly being deployed to predict, prevent and manage injury risk in professional sport. Injury prevention has made significant advancements in recent years as clubs attempt to alleviate the risk of injury that overexertion and inadequate rest present. GPS trackers, wearable sensors and optical tracking systems collect real‑time data which machine‑learning models then analyze to identify patterns associated with increased injury risk, often before clinical symptoms develop.

item.title: Sun Life taps Medzown to steer high-cost members into clinical trials
item.description: Sun Life US is teaming up with Medzown, a precision medicine company, to connect employees at self-insured employers with clinical trials for cancer and other complex conditions. Medzown uses an AI-driven model to flag members facing a cancer diagnosis, rare disease, or complex ortho/MSK condition who might benefit from advanced treatment — including enrollment in a clinical trial — and then routes them there with the help of precision-medicine scientists and personal navigators. Per Sun Life’s 2026 high-cost claims report, cancer runs about $252,000 per person per year and ortho/MSK about $120,000.

item.title: Bed Bath & Beyond buys Fathom
item.description: Bed Bath & Beyond has agreed to acquire Fathom Holdings , a tech-driven real estate services platform that bundles brokerage, mortgage, title, insurance, and SaaS under its proprietary intelliAgent software. Martha Stewart’s Hint is chasing the same “single relationship for the whole home” thesis from the management side; Bed Bath & Beyond is buying its way there through Fathom. The deal feeds Bed Bath & Beyond’s “Everything Home” strategy — an attempt to build what the company calls the nation’s first end-to-end homeownership platform, organized around three pillars: Homeownership & Transactions, Omnichannel Commerce, and Home Services.

item.title: Progressive reports 82.1% CR for May
item.description: Progressive released its financial results for May, ending the month with a combined ratio of 82.1% and net income of ~$1.4 billion. Year-to-date, Progressive’s net income stands at $5.3 billion and combined ratio sits at 86.3%. During the month, Progressive added around 203k policies to its overall count.

item.title: María José Rodríguez (Lagun Aro) entra en el Consejo de AMICE y Jorge Vázquez (Mutua Madrileña) es nombrado vicepresidente
item.description: Entre ellos se encuentra María José Rodríguez, directora general de Seguros Lagun Aro, quien sustituirá en el órgano de gobierno a Pablo Mongelos. AMICE (Association of Mutual Insurers and Insurance Cooperatives in Europe) ha anunciado la incorporación de dos nuevos miembros a su Consejo de Administración.

item.title: La Mutua de los Ingenieros crece en primas, provisiones y mutualistas en 2025
item.description: La Asamblea General Ordinaria de La Mutua de los Ingenieros ha aprobado los resultados correspondientes a 2025, ejercicio en el que la aseguradora alcanzó un volumen de primas de 23,7 millones de euros, un 7% más que en 2023, y un volumen de prestaciones superior a los 12,3 millones.

item.title: Cojebro y Segurlike estrenan oficinas centrales en el corazón financiero de Madrid
item.description: Alrededor de un centenar de profesionales, entre ellos directivos de aseguradoras y de algunas de las principales instituciones del sector, participaron ayer en la inauguración de las nuevas oficinas centrales de Cojebro y Segurlike.

item.title: Home Insurance And The Unraveling of Florida Communities
item.description: Petersburg, Florida, in November 2024, I found myself walking down a quiet residential street in Shore Acres, a low-lying, bayfront neighborhood not far from where I grew up. For over a decade I’ve been researching how property markets adapt to the changing financial realities of climate change in Florida, the Netherlands and beyond. On this street, I saw a community slowly unraveling as climate shocks – and the subsequent market responses to them – have reshaped the cost of living.

item.title: AI Is Reshaping Insurance: What Claims Pros and Lawyers Must Know Now
item.description: Major insurance carriers like State Farm, Allstate, Progressive, Liberty Mutual, Nationwide and USAA have already begun implementing AI in day-to-day underwriting and claims operation activities. According to the JD Power Auto Claims Satisfaction Study , it takes an average of 22.3 days for a car insurance claim to be resolved. Home insurance claims take longer , around 23.9 days.

item.title: Brokers back fire levy review, call insurance funding model an ‘unfair burden’
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: ACCC approves Zurich-Beazley deal, finds ‘unlikely’ threat to competition
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: Underwriters far from returning to pre-war war risk pricing even as US-Iran deal looms, brokers say
item.description: To view the full article and get unlimited access to InsuranceAsia News's exclusive insight across 5,000+ articles

item.title: USAA extends $95m of Res Re 2022-1 cat bond as losses from 24/25 risk period develop
item.description: Insurer USAA has extended the maturity dates for two tranches of its Residential Reinsurance 2022 Limited (Series 2022-1) annual aggregate catastrophe bond issuance, amounting to $95 million of notes, which we assume is to allow for continued development of losses from the April 2024 to end of March 2025 risk period. These cat bond notes were exposed to multiple catastrophe and severe weather events through the annual risk period that ran from April 1st 2024, which had already seen them considered at-risk of losses and marked down on secondary market cat bond pricing sheets. All four tranches of notes of USAA’s Residential Re 2022-1 catastrophe bond were scheduled for maturity on June 6th 2026.

item.title: Arch raises Ramble Re 2026-1 retro cat bond target to $150m, as pricing falls
item.description: Initially, the target was to secure $100 million of peak peril catastrophe retrocessional reinsurance from the offering of Ramble Re 2026-1 cat bond notes. Arch Capital Group is now aiming to upsize its latest catastrophe bond sponsorship, now targeting a 50% upsized $150 million of property catastrophe retrocession for peak North American perils from its new Ramble Re Ltd. For comparison, the Ramble Re 2024-1 cat bond notes from Arch’s last cat bond sponsorship had an initial expected loss of 3.19% and priced to pay investors a spread of 6.25%.

item.title: Mercury sets $150m Cal fire reinsurance target as price falls again for new Luca Re cat bond
item.description: Mercury General Corporation (Mercury Insurance), the California headquartered insurer, has now set the target for fire reinsurance limit from the new Luca Re Ltd. The $150 million of Series 2026-1 cat bond notes that Luca Re is offering to investors will provide Mercury with a roughly three-year source of collateralized reinsurance protection against wildfire and fire-following earthquake losses in the state of California, on an indemnity trigger and per-occurrence basis. Mercury Insurance ventured back to the catastrophe bond market at the end of May , with an initial target to secure $100 million of California wildfire and fire following earthquake protection from the capital markets from what will become its second 144A cat bond sponsorship.

item.title: Aon sees no immediate shift in insurer appetite following Iran-US agreement
item.description: and Iran have reached an agreement to halt their military conflict, uncertainty surrounding the deal’s terms is likely to prevent any material improvement in risk appetite for the sector in the near term. The preliminary agreement establishes a 60-day negotiating period during which the parties will seek to conclude a comprehensive peace treaty. A formal signing ceremony for the framework agreement is scheduled to take place on June 19 in Bürgenstock, Switzerland, near Geneva.
